w:r.w:t: Your Physio. In Your Pocket. Offline.

PhysioPath is an offline-first physiotherapy exercise guidance web app built for two distinct user types: physiotherapists who prescribe exercise plans, and patients who need to follow them reliably at home — with no internet, no login, and no confusion.
The Core Problem We Are Solving
Patients prescribed physio exercises post-injury or surgery often perform them incorrectly at home
They forget clinic demonstrations within days, leading to re-injury from improper form
Existing solutions require cameras (too complex), constant internet (unavailable at home), or patient logins (too much friction)
There is no guided, offline-capable tool that bridges the therapist and patient without technical barriers

PhysioPath solves all of this with a therapist-side plan builder and a patient-side guided workout experience — featuring animated exercise cards, voice coaching, rep tracking, and a weekly progress log. All of it works after a single online setup, with zero internet required thereafter.
The physiotherapy compliance gap is a well-documented clinical issue. Patients receive hands-on demonstration at the clinic but have no reliable reference at home. The result:
Adherence drops sharply after one week
Therapists have no visibility into patient compliance
No current tool is simple enough for non-technical patients in low-connectivity environments
No real-time camera pose detection — too compute-heavy for 24-hour build
No requiring patients to create an account or log in
No requiring internet connection after the initial setup
No requiring therapist to have technical skills to create a plan
Therapist creates exercise plans via a simple form-based interface
Patient accesses plan via a shareable link — no app install, no login
Clear visual exercise steps with common mistake warnings
Rep tracking and weekly log stored locally on device
Moderate — comfortable with forms and email
Low — uses WhatsApp and basic browsing
Assign correct exercises and ensure adherence
No way to know if patient is doing exercises
Forgets steps, loses printed sheets, unsure of form
Weak or no home internet — often offline
The therapist creates a plan using a clean web form — no training required. The form captures:
Patient name and plan duration (in weeks)
Exercise list with: name, muscle group, sets, reps, rest time between sets, difficulty level
Per exercise: step-by-step illustrated instructions (image upload or pre-built SVG library)
Per exercise: a list of Common Mistakes shown as red-flagged warnings to the patient

A library of 15+ common physiotherapy exercises with pre-made SVG illustrations and default mistake warnings. The therapist can select from the library and customise reps/sets, reducing plan creation time to under 5 minutes.
On saving a plan, the system generates:
A QR code the therapist can print or show on screen — patient scans once
Optional plan expiry date (e.g. 4 weeks), after which the plan shows a 'Plan Complete' screen
The patient lands on their personalised plan page. Each exercise is presented as a card showing:
Exercise name and muscle group targeted
Common mistakes section — highlighted in red with warning icon
Swipe left/right to navigate between exercises
The flagship feature. The patient taps 'Start Workout' and enters a step-by-step guided session:
State machine flow: Intro → Exercise Step View → Rep Counter → Rest Timer → Next Exercise → Summary
Voice Cues via Web Speech API: 'Starting Set 1 of 3', 'Rep 5 — watch your knee alignment', 'Rest for 30 seconds'
Large tap-to-count rep button — usable with one hand, designed for patients in pain
Rest countdown timer between sets with audio alert
Wake Lock API — screen stays on throughout the workout, no phone going dark mid-exercise
Animated step transitions — smooth, not jarring
All progress auto-saved to device storage after each set
Weekly completion heatmap — GitHub-style calendar showing which days exercises were done
Streak counter — 'Day 5 streak 🔥' shown on home screen for motivation
Per-exercise completion log: which sets were done, how many reps
All stored in IndexedDB — works fully offline, no server sync needed
Progressive Web App — patient can tap 'Add to Home Screen' for app-like experience
Service Worker caches all app assets and exercise plan data on first load
All subsequent visits work with zero internet, including in airplane mode
iOS install guidance shown automatically for iPhone users (Share → Add to Home Screen)
'Available Offline ✅' badge shown once the plan is cached
Service worker, asset caching, offline shell
Store plan data and progress logs on device
API server for plan creation and retrieval
Generate patient QR on plan save
Free tier, fast deploy in under 10 min
Step 1 — Therapist logs in and creates exercise plan via form → saved to MongoDB with unique token
Step 2 — System generates link (physiopath.app/patient/{token}) and QR code → therapist shares with patient
Step 3 — Patient opens link once (needs internet) → app fetches plan from API → saves to IndexedDB on device
Step 4 — Patient returns for daily workouts → app loads from IndexedDB → fully offline from this point
Step 5 — Patient marks reps done → progress saved to IndexedDB → weekly log updated locally
Step 6 — (Optional) When online, progress synced back to server via background sync API
exercises: [{ id, name, muscleGroup, sets, reps, restSeconds,
Create patient plan — returns token + QR code URL
Patient fetches plan by token (cached after first call)
Sync patient weekly log when back online
Fetch pre-built exercise library for therapist
All buttons minimum 64×64px — patients may be in pain
Nav bar fixed at bottom for thumb reach (not top)
Wake Lock API — screen never sleeps mid-exercise
Swipe left/right between exercise cards via react-swipeable
Minimum 16px body, 20px+ for instructions — usable without glasses
Green '✅ Available Offline' badge shown in header after cache
Custom banner with Share → Add to Home Screen instructions for Safari
active:scale-95 CSS transition on all interactive elements
Header: Patient name, week number, streak counter (🔥 N day streak)
Today's Plan section: Exercise cards with set completion indicators (✅ ✅ ⬜)
Swipe hint: < swipe for next exercise >
Primary CTA: Large 'START WORKOUT' button — full width, green
Bottom Nav: Home | Workout | Progress | Settings
Top bar: Exercise N of Total, exercise name, X button to exit
Illustration area: SVG/image of exercise step
Step text: Large readable instruction (e.g. 'Hold for 5 seconds')
Mistake warning: Red banner '⚠️ Don't lock your knee' shown during relevant steps
Set indicator: 'SET 2 OF 3' centered above rep button
Rep button: Giant circular button showing current rep count — tap to increment
Bottom: Rest timer appears automatically between sets
Repo setup, Vite + React PWA scaffold, MongoDB Atlas cluster, Express skeleton
Backend: auth, plan creation, token generation, QR code, GET plan route
Patient UI: plan load from token, exercise card component, steps + mistakes display
Guided Workout Mode: state machine, rep tracker, rest timer, voice cues
Dexie.js offline storage, service worker config, offline badge, PWA manifest
Therapist portal: login, exercise builder form, library picker, plan save + QR display
Progress heatmap, streak counter, Wake Lock API, iOS install prompt
Mobile QA: test on real Android + iPhone, fix touch targets, test offline in DevTools
Deploy to Vercel + Render, record demo video, prepare judge presentation
No Other Team Will Have This Because
Audio cues guide each rep, set, and rest period — feels like a real physio
Requires Web Speech API integration + timing logic most teams skip
Works in airplane mode after first open — demonstrated live to judges
Requires PWA + Service Worker + IndexedDB — complex to implement in 24hr
Patient scans QR at clinic — one tap to start, zero friction
Most teams will use manual link sharing or require app install
Motivational daily streak shown on home screen — drives adherence
Behaviour-change psychology baked into the product — not just a feature list
Red-flagged common mistakes shown at the exact moment of each step
Directly targets the re-injury problem — not just exercise display
Screen never sleeps during workout — no phone going dark mid-exercise
Small but powerful — judges will notice when demoed on a real phone
Active coaching experience, not passive card reading
Transforms the app from an information tool to a workout companion
Test offline mode in Chrome DevTools from Hour 17 — fix before Hour 22 QA
Voice cues not triggering on iOS (requires user gesture)
Show 'Tap to enable audio' prompt before workout starts on iOS
Wake Lock not supported on all devices
Graceful fallback — show 'Keep screen on in phone settings' tip
MongoDB Atlas free tier cold start
Ping endpoint on app load to warm it up; all patient reads use cache anyway
Therapist form too complex to use in 24hr build
Simplify to just 5 fields + pre-built library; full custom steps as bonus only
PWA install banner not showing on iPhone
Detect iOS user agent and show custom manual install instructions banner
What We Will Demo Live to the Judges
Turn on airplane mode → open the patient link → app loads fully offline — no spinner, no error
Tap 'Start Workout' → voice says 'Starting Set 1 of 3, exercise: Straight Leg Raise'
Tap rep button 10 times → rest timer counts down with audio alert
Complete full workout → summary screen shows sets done → progress saved to heatmap
Open therapist portal → create a new plan in under 3 minutes → QR code generated
Scan QR on a second device → patient plan loads instantly
Use this word-for-word during the presentation:
"The problem is simple: patients forget, and they re-injure themselves. So watch this."
Turn on airplane mode on your phone — show it to the judges.
Open physiopath.app/patient/demo — it loads instantly from cache.
Tap 'Start Workout' — voice says the exercise name and set number out loud.
Show the mistake warning banner: 'Don't arch your lower back'.
Finish the set — rest timer counts down. Screen stays on the entire time.
Open therapist portal on laptop — create a plan and generate a QR code in 2 minutes.
Scan QR with a judge's phone — patient plan loads. No login. No app install. Done.
"No camera. No login. No internet. Just physio that works. That's PhysioPath."
The following 15 exercises will be available in the therapist's library at launch:
Your physio. In your pocket. Offline.
